behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counts + thresholds) with no required OS/network/subprocess/LLM behavior. MOCK-MISS check: this behavior does not require cross-module handoff to fail. Adversarial critique: if tokenization/config loading were external, Unit could miss integration bugs; rejected because behavior text scopes to analyzer assignment logic itself. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on configured word lists and assignment rule, not transport/runtime infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Pure unit test of the scoring function with table-driven cases using in-memory configs and reviews containing only listed words; assert exact sentiment label at boundary counts and off-by-one thresholds.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on content quality from the real explanation generator (phrase-grounded reasoning). A stubbed/mocked model would hide failures where outputs become generic. Adversarial critique: if analyzer were deterministic extractive templates, Integration could suffice; rejected because behavior explicitly requires contextual natural-language reasoning quality. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this is a single analyzer output step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test against the real explanation analyzer/model: submit reviews with distinctive phrases, assert explanation includes grounded references to those phrases (or close paraphrases) and fails when output is generic boilerplate; run multiple trials to catch non-deterministic drift.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic tie-break logic in the keyword analyzer with in-memory configuration; no real infra or model output required. Adversarial critique: if tie-break were applied after service-layer normalization, Integration could be needed; rejected because behavior text targets sentiment score rule itself. Rejection-gate: higher levels disqualified since no OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test matrix over mixed-keyword inputs and tie conditions with configurable tie-break settings; assert exact resulting sentiment for each rule variant and threshold edge.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm interpretation is model-judgment quality; deterministic mocks cannot validate whether intent is correctly inferred. REAL-INFRA may exist, but the decisive risk is AI output correctness, so Agentic is the minimum. Adversarial critique: a handcrafted sarcasm rule engine could be Integration; rejected because behavior says analyzer interprets context and provides natural-language reasoning, implying model-like semantic judgment. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because only one agentic inference is required here.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation set with sarcastic reviews containing positive tokens; call real explanation analyzer and assert explanations identify negative intent with supporting rationale, tracking pass-rate across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration plus semantic alignment: explanation must match score direction. Integration catches packaging/contract issues, but a mocked explanation can falsely pass alignment that fails with real model output. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) yes, correctness depends on real AI output alignment, so upgrade to Agentic. Adversarial critique: if explanation were deterministically derived from score, Integration would be enough; rejected because behavior requires explanation content to reference sentiment direction, and explanations are generated by a separate contextual analyzer. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because only one agentic stage (explanation generation) is involved, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end service call using real explanation analyzer: assert response contains both fields atomically and that explanation sentiment direction (via rubric or judge check) agrees with returned score across positive/negative/neutral cases.
